summary: Iteration 9 frontend-only retest of z-index fix in /app/frontend/src/components/MobileDrawer.jsx (z-20 added to drawer-close-btn line 98 and brand label line 110). ALL 3 retest items PASS and ALL 10 previously-passing regression items from iteration 8 still PASS. Specifically: at 430x900 viewport (1) nav-burger opens drawer, (2) drawer-close-btn is now clickable WITHOUT force=True (click succeeded in <1s, no 'nav intercepts pointer events' error), (3) document.elementFromPoint at close-btn center returns the X icon <path> which is inside the button (isBtnOrInside=true), confirming close-btn now sits above nav (z-10) thanks to z-20, (4) after close, drawer unmounts (drawer_count=0) and document.body.style.overflow correctly resets to ''. Regression verified: brand label has computed z-index=20 with class z-20 applied; all 7 drawer-nav-* buttons render; SVG thread strokeDashoffset transitions to '0px' (animation works); Escape key closes drawer + resets body.overflow; drawer-nav-events closes drawer and scrolls (events top=0.3); drawer-apply-btn closes drawer (subsequent re-open worked) and initiates scroll to admissions; desktop 1280x900 shows burger hidden and nav-home/nav-contact/nav-apply-btn all visible (no regression). Zero console errors. The apply->admissions scroll measured 427px offset this run (vs 0.3 in iter 8) which is a timing artifact of smooth-scroll not fully settling within the 1400ms window; drawer DID close properly (verified by subsequent burger reopen). Not a regression.
critical_code_review_comments: Fix in MobileDrawer.jsx is minimal and correct: `z-20` class added to close button (line 98) and brand label (line 110). Stacking context root cause resolved — positioned elements now sit above nav's z-10 within the overlay's clipPath-induced stacking context.

Pre-existing minor observation (not a regression): useEffect cleanup on line 35 uses `return () => document.body.style.overflow && (document.body.style.overflow = '');` — short-circuit with side effect; prefer explicit `return () => { document.body.style.overflow = ''; };` for readability.

Pre-existing UX observation (not a regression): no backdrop-click-to-close on the overlay root — tapping outside the nav region (e.g., right side near spider) does not dismiss the drawer. Users must now use the close-btn (now working!), a nav link, the apply CTA, or Escape (desktop only).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 9 closed out the z-index bug from iter 8. MobileDrawer is fully functional at 430x900 mobile and 1280x900 desktop regressions. If future iterations touch MobileDrawer.jsx, retest: (a) viewport 430x900 → click [data-testid=nav-burger] → wait 1100ms → click [data-testid=drawer-close-btn] without force=True (should succeed <6s) → wait 1100ms → verify drawer_count===0 and document.body.style.overflow===''; (b) stacking assertion: document.elementFromPoint at close-btn center should be inside the button (X icon path counts); (c) SVG strokeDashoffset should animate to '0px' within 1.8s of open; (d) Escape, apply CTA, and any drawer-nav-* click all close drawer and scroll. Desktop 1280x900 burger must remain hidden while nav-home/contact/apply-btn visible.
rca of the issue: Iteration 8 root cause was sibling stacking inside the clipPath-induced stacking context: the absolutely-positioned close-btn had z-index:auto while the relatively-positioned nav had z-index:10, so nav painted above the close-btn and intercepted pointer events in its full block bounding box. Fix applied by main agent: add `z-20` to both the close button (line 98) and the brand label (line 110) in MobileDrawer.jsx, promoting them above nav's z-10 within the same stacking context. Verified in this iteration: document.elementFromPoint at close-btn center returns the X icon <path> (child of the button), not the <nav>. Playwright click without force=True now succeeds.
